questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A nonconducting disc of radius R and uniform positive surface charge density $\sigma$ is placed on the ground with its axis vertical. A particle of specific charge $\frac{\mathrm{q}}{\mathrm{m}}=\frac{4 \epsilon_0 \mathrm{~g}}{\sigma}$ is dropped along the axis of the disc from a height $h$. The value of $h$ if the particle just reaches the disc is $\left(2 \sqrt{\frac{R}{3}}\right)^m$. Determine the value of m .
questions.solution: $\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{p}}=\frac{\sigma}{2 \epsilon_0}\left[\sqrt{\mathrm{R}^2+\mathrm{h}^2}-\mathrm{h}\right] ; \mathrm{V}_0=\frac{\sigma \mathrm{R}}{2 \epsilon_0}$ To just reach mgh $\begin{aligned} & =q\left[V_0-V_p\right] \Rightarrow \frac{g h \sigma}{4 \epsilon_0 g}=\frac{\sigma}{2 \epsilon_0}\left[R-\sqrt{R^2+h^2}+h\right] \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{h}{2}=R+h-\sqrt{R^2+h^2} \Rightarrow \sqrt{R^2+h^2}=R+\frac{h}{2} \\ & \Rightarrow R^2+h^2=R^2+\frac{h^2}{4}+R h \Rightarrow \frac{3 h^2}{4}=R h \Rightarrow h=\frac{4}{3} R=\left(2 \sqrt{\frac{R}{3}}\right)^2 \Rightarrow m=2 \end{aligned}$ OR Check dimensionally $h=\left(2 \sqrt{\frac{R}{3}}\right)^m \Rightarrow m=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Four identical point charges are fixed at the four corners of a square of a side length I. Another charged particle of mass m and charge $+q$ is projected towards centre of square from a large distance along the line perpendicular to plane of square. The minimum value of initial velocity $v_0$ required to cross the square is? $(\mathrm{m}=1 \mathrm{gm}, \mathrm{l}=4 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~m}, \mathrm{Q}=1 \mu \mathrm{c}, \mathrm{q}=0.5 \mu \mathrm{c})$
questions.solution:
Particle will cross the square if it crosses the point of maximum potential between A and C -which is $C$ itself.
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{1}{2} m v_0^2=K+\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0} \frac{4 Q^2 q}{(I / \sqrt{2})} \\
& \mathrm{K}\gt0 \Rightarrow V_0^2=8 \sqrt{2}\left(\frac{1}{4 \pi \varepsilon_0}\right)\left(\frac{\mathrm{Qq}}{\mathrm{ml}}\right)
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A rod of finite length having linear charge density $\lambda$ subtends an angle of $15^{\circ}$ at a point P which is also the centre of curvature of a circular arc having same linear charge density $\lambda$ as shown in figure. If the net electric field at the origin is $\frac{b K \lambda}{R}$. Find the value of b :
questions.solution:
formula for rod and are both are
$\frac{k \lambda}{d}(\sin \alpha+\sin \beta) \hat{i}+\frac{k \lambda}{d}(\cos \alpha-\cos \beta) \hat{j}$

So, $\vec{E}=\overrightarrow{0}$ and $b=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A ball of mass 2 kg having charge $1 \mu \mathrm{C}$ is dropped from the top of a very high tower. In space electric field exist in horizontal direction away from tower which varies as $E=(5-2 x) \times 10^6 \mathrm{~V} / \mathrm{m}$ (where x is horizontal distance from tower). Find the maximum horizontal distance (in $m$ ) ball can go from the tower. (no air resistance)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Conducting shell of radius $R$ carrying charge $Q$, another charge $Q$ is placed at $3 R$ distance from centre of shell. If the potential at point $P$ due to charge on shell is $\frac{11}{3 n} \frac{K Q}{R}$, Then the value of $n$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & V_O=V_P \\ & \frac{K Q}{R}+\frac{K Q}{3 R}=V_{\text {shell }}+\frac{2 K q}{5 R} \\ & V_{\text {shell }}=\frac{K Q}{R}\left[1+\frac{1}{3}-\frac{2}{5}\right] \\ & =\frac{K Q}{R}\left[\frac{4}{3}-\frac{2}{5}\right] \\ & =\frac{K Q}{R}\left[\frac{20-9}{15}\right]=\frac{11}{15} \frac{K Q}{R}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A thin insulating rod is hinged about one of its ends. It can rotate on a smooth horizonal surface. The charge density on the rod is defined as $\lambda=15 \mathrm{x}^2, 0 \lt \mathrm{x} \leq \frac{\ell}{2}=-\mathrm{bx}^{\mathrm{n}}, \frac{\ell}{2} \lt \mathrm{x} \leq \ell$ where b is a positive constant. An electric field $\mathrm{E}_0$ in the horizontal direction and perpendicular to the rod is switched on. Find the value of $(b+n)^2$, if the rod has to remain stationary.
questions.solution: For $0 \lt \mathrm{x} \lt \frac{\ell}{2}, \mathrm{~d} \tau_1=\mathrm{E}_0(\lambda \mathrm{dx}) \mathrm{x}$ $\Rightarrow \tau_1=\int_0^{\ell / 2} \mathrm{E}_0\left(15 \mathrm{x}^2 \mathrm{dx}\right) \mathrm{x}=15 \mathrm{E}_0 \int_0^{\ell / 2} \mathrm{x}^3 \mathrm{dx}=\frac{15 \mathrm{E}_0}{4}\left(\frac{\ell}{4}\right)^4=\frac{15 \mathrm{E}_0 \ell^4}{64}$ For $\frac{1}{2} \lt x \lt \ell, d \tau_2=E_0\left(b x^n d x\right) x \Rightarrow \tau_2=\int_{/ / 2} E_0 b x^{n+1} d x=\frac{E_0 b}{n+2} \frac{\left(2^{n+2}-1\right)}{2^{n+2}} \ell^{n+2}$ Now According to question $\tau_1=\tau_2 \Rightarrow \frac{15 \mathrm{E}_0 \ell^4}{64}=\frac{\mathrm{E}_0 \mathrm{~b}\left(2^{\mathrm{n}+2}-1\right) \ell^{n+2}}{(\mathrm{n}+2) 2^{\mathrm{n}+2}}$ $\Rightarrow 4=\mathrm{n}+2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{n}=2$ and $\mathrm{b}=1$ Therefore $(\mathrm{a}+\mathrm{b})^2=(2+1)^2=9$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A positive charged sphere of mass $\mathrm{m}=5 \mathrm{~kg}$ is attached by a spring of force constant $\mathrm{K}=10^4 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m}$. The sphere is tied with a thread so that the spring is in its natural length. Another identical, negatively charged sphere is fixed to the floor, vertically below the positively charged sphere as shown in figure. Initial seperation between spheres is $x_0=0.5 \mathrm{~m}$. Now thread is burnt and maximum elongation of the spring is 0.1 m . If charge on each sphere is $(\alpha \times 25) \mu \mathrm{C}$ then find the value of $\alpha$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{K}=10^4 \mathrm{~N} / \mathrm{m} ; \mathrm{m}=5 \mathrm{~kg} \\ & U_i=\frac{-k q^2}{(0.5)} \\ & U_f=\frac{-k q^2}{0.4}-m g(0.1)+\frac{1}{2} k x^2 \\ & \mathrm{U}_{\mathrm{i}}+\mathrm{k}_{\mathrm{i}}=\mathrm{U}_{\mathrm{f}}+k f_{f^{-}} \Rightarrow\left(\mathrm{kq}^2\right)(2)=\mathrm{kq}^2(2.5)+\mathrm{mg}(0.1)-\frac{1}{2} k x^2 \\ & \frac{1}{2} k x^2=\frac{k q^2}{2}+\mathrm{mg}(0.1) \\ & \frac{1}{2}\left(10^2\right) \times\left(\frac{1}{100}\right)=\frac{\left(9 \times 10^9\right) \times q^2}{2}+\frac{45 \times 2}{590 \times 10^9}=q^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{q}^2=\frac{5 \times 2}{10^9}=10^{-8} \Rightarrow \theta=100 \mu \mathrm{C} \Rightarrow \alpha \times 25=100 \Rightarrow \alpha=4\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics

questions.q: A positively charged particle starts at rest 25 cm from a second positively charged particle which is held stationary throughout the experiment. The first particle is released and accelerates directly away from the second particle. When the first particle has moved 25 cm , it has reached a velocity of $10 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. What is the maximum velocity ( $\mathrm{in} \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$ ) that the first particle will reach ?
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A hollow non conducting sphere A and a solid non conducting sphere $B$ of equal radius ' $R$ ' and masses $m$ and $2 m$ are kept at a large distance apart on a rough horizontal surface. Charge on the two spheres A and B are Q and -2 Q respectively. Charges are distributed uniformly and remain constant and uniform as the spheres come closer. Friction is sufficient to support pure rolling and the kinetic energy of the two spheres just before collision is $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{A}}$ and $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{B}}$. Find $100 \times \frac{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{A}}}{\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{B}}}$.
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{v}_1$ and $\mathrm{v}_2$ be the speeds of sphere A and B just before collision, then from conservation of energy,
$\begin{aligned}
& 0=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}_1^2\left(1+\frac{2}{3}\right)+\frac{1}{2}(2 \mathrm{~m}) v_2^2\left(1+\frac{2}{5}\right)+\frac{\mathrm{K} \cdot \mathrm{Q}(-2 \mathrm{Q})}{2 \mathrm{R}} \\
& 0=\frac{5}{6} \mathrm{mv}_1^2+\frac{7}{5} \mathrm{mv}_2^2-\frac{\mathrm{K} \cdot \mathrm{Q}^2}{\mathrm{R}} ; \frac{5}{6} \mathrm{mv}_1^2+\frac{7}{5} \mathrm{mv}_2^2=\frac{\mathrm{K} \cdot \mathrm{Q}^2}{\mathrm{R}}
\end{aligned}$

From conservation of angular momentum about any point ' O '
$0=\mathrm{mv}_1 \mathrm{R}+\frac{2}{3} \mathrm{mR}^2 \cdot\left(\frac{\mathrm{v}_1}{\mathrm{R}}\right)-2 \mathrm{mv}_2 \mathrm{R}-\frac{2}{5}(2 \mathrm{~m}) \mathrm{r}^2\left(\frac{\mathrm{v}^2}{\mathrm{R}}\right) ; \mathrm{v}_1=\frac{42}{25} \mathrm{v}_2 \ldots$

Solving equation (i) and (ii) $\mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{B}}=\frac{25 \mathrm{KQ}^2}{67 \mathrm{R}} ; \mathrm{K}_{\mathrm{A}}=\frac{42 \mathrm{KQ}^2}{67 \mathrm{R}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A charge $+Q$ is located somewhere inside a vertical cone such that the depth of the charge from the free surface of the cone is H . It is found that the flux associated with the cone with the curved surface is $\frac{3 Q}{5 \varepsilon_0}$. If the charge is raised vertically through a height 2 H , the flux associated with the cone with the curved surface is $\frac{\eta Q}{20 \varepsilon_0}$. What should be the value of $\eta$ ?
questions.solution: If we place another similar cone on this one, net flux $=\frac{Q}{\varepsilon_0}$ $\Rightarrow$ Flux with the upper cone $=\frac{Q}{\varepsilon_0}-\frac{3 Q}{5 \varepsilon_0}=\frac{2 Q}{5 \varepsilon_0}=\frac{8 Q}{20 \varepsilon_0}$. This must also be the flux associated with the lower cone when the charge is raised through a height 2 H because of symmetry.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
